title: ConfigurationRows Object
description: Returns a collection of the rows in the table. The header row is not included in this list.

methods.name: add
methods.description: Adds a new row to the table. For the top table, this creates a new configuration. For theme tables, this creates a new theme. The new row is added to the bottom of the table, and the cell values are copied from the row above it. You can also use the ConfigurationRow.copy method to create a new row by copying any existing row.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: item
methods.description: A method that returns the specified row using an index into the collection. These are returned in the same order as in the table; the first row is the default row.

methods.name: itemById
methods.description: A method that returns the row with the specified ID.

methods.name: itemByName
methods.description: A method that returns the row with the specified name.

properties.name: count
properties.description: Returns the number of rows in the table where the header row is not included.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

add.title: ConfigurationRows.add Method
add.description: Adds a new row to the table. For the top table, this creates a new configuration. For theme tables, this creates a new theme. The new row is added to the bottom of the table, and the cell values are copied from the row above it. You can also use the ConfigurationRow.copy method to create a new row by copying any existing row.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The name of the new row. An empty string can be provided, which will cause Fusion to use a default naming scheme to create a name.

classType.title: ConfigurationRows.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

item.title: ConfigurationRows.item Method
item.description: A method that returns the specified row using an index into the collection. These are returned in the same order as in the table; the first row is the default row.
item.return_description: Returns the specified row or null if an invalid index was specified.

parameters.name: index
parameters.description: The index of the row to return, where the first row is index 0. The headers do not count as a row.

itemById.title: ConfigurationRows.itemById Method
itemById.description: A method that returns the row with the specified ID.
itemById.return_description: Returns the specified row or null if a row with the specified ID does not exist.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The id of the row to return.

itemByName.title: ConfigurationRows.itemByName Method
itemByName.description: A method that returns the row with the specified name.
itemByName.return_description: Returns the specified row or null if the named row does not exist.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The name of the row to return.

count.title: ConfigurationRows.count Property
count.description: Returns the number of rows in the table where the header row is not included.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = configurationRows_var.count

isValid.title: ConfigurationRows.isValid Property
isValid.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = configurationRows_var.isValid

objectType.title: ConfigurationRows.objectType Property
objectType.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object. It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = configurationRows_var.objectType
